facts.text: East Williston was founded in the late 1800s, and its agricultural roots still shape the local economy and identity.
The town’s nickname, the “Cucumber Capital of the World,” reflects a long history of farming, ranching, and horse‑related businesses.
Devil’s Den Prehistoric Spring, a privately operated spring inside a collapsed cave, offers a unique snorkeling and scuba diving experience with crystal‑clear water.
Water temperatures at Devil’s Den stay around 72°F year‑round, making it a comfortable spot for water adventures.
East Williston sits within the Gainesville, Florida Metropolitan Statistical Area, linking it to a larger regional community.
The long‑running Williston Peanut Festival celebrates the town’s agricultural heritage and draws visitors to its annual festivities.
